label: PandaDoc
description: Create, send, and track documents and collect e-signatures through PandaDoc's document workflow platform.

form.title: Connect PandaDoc
form.subtitle: Paste your PandaDoc API key to start creating and tracking documents.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste a PandaDoc API key — generated in Settings → API and integrations. No OAuth setup required, works instantly.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via API Key

PandaDoc provides a personal API key you can copy from your account settings in under a minute.

### Steps

1. Log in to your PandaDoc account at [app.pandadoc.com](https://app.pandadoc.com).
2. Click your profile avatar in the top-right corner and choose **Settings**.
3. In the left sidebar, navigate to **Integrations → API & webhooks** (or **API and integrations**).
4. Click **Generate API Key** if you don't already have one, or copy the existing key shown.
5. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

### Notes

- The key is scoped to your account — documents and templates accessible to your user will be available to Anton.
- If you regenerate the key in PandaDoc, paste the new value here to refresh the connection.
- Sandbox keys (from the Developer Dashboard) have a different prefix; use a production key for real documents.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in PandaDoc Settings → Integrations → API & webhooks. Treat this like a password.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0
methods.description: Authorize Anton via OAuth 2.0 using your own PandaDoc OAuth application. Suitable for multi-user or production integrations where per-user token scoping is required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OAuth 2.0

OAuth 2.0 requires you to create a PandaDoc OAuth application to get a client ID and secret.

### Steps

1. Go to the [PandaDoc Developer Dashboard](https://developers.pandadoc.com/) and log in.
2. Navigate to **My Applications → Create Application**.
3. Fill in the app name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (or the URI Anton instructs you to use).
4. After saving, copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the application detail page.
5. Paste both values into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. A browser window will open for you to approve access. Log in with the PandaDoc account you want Anton to use.

### Notes

- The client secret is shown once at creation time in some plans — store it safely before leaving the page.
- Scopes `read+write` grant access to documents, templates, contacts, and webhooks.
- To revoke access later, delete the application or remove it under your account's authorized apps.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From the PandaDoc Developer Dashboard → My Applications → your app's detail page.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID. Shown at app creation time — copy it before leaving the page.
